content.Text: [Tue 2026-04-21 20:24 PDT] Add a new "Smart Conversation Memory" module to the agenticchat app. This is a cross-session memory system that autonomously extracts facts, decisions, preferences, and action items from conversations and surfaces relevant memories when starting new chats. ## Architecture This is a single-file browser app. ALL JavaScript goes in `app.js` as an IIFE module (revealing module pattern). ALL CSS goes in `style.css`. The HTML button goes in `index.html` toolbar. ## What to build ### 1. Add to `app.js` - append a new IIFE module called `ConversationMemory` The module should: **Memory Extraction (autonomous):** - After each assistant response, scan the conversation for extractable memories - Extract these memory types: - `fact` - factual statements ("I'm using Python 3.11", "My project is called Atlas") - `decision` - decisions made ("Let's use PostgreSQL for the database") - `preference` - user preferences ("I prefer concise answers", "I like TypeScript over JavaScript") - `action` - action items ("I need to refactor the auth module", "TODO: add tests") - `insight` - key insights or learnings ("The bottleneck was in the serialization layer") - Use heuristic pattern matching (regex + keyword detection), NOT API calls - Each memory has: `{id, type, content, source: {sessionId, sessionName, timestamp}, confidence, tags[]}` **Pattern matching rules:** - Facts: statements with "I am", "I use", "I have", "my X is", "we use", "our X" - Decisions: "let's", "we'll", "decided to", "going with", "I'll use", "the plan is" - Preferences: "I prefer", "I like", "I want", "better to", "I always", "I never" - Actions: "TODO", "need to", "should", "will do", "don't forget", "remember to", "action item" - Insights: "the reason", "turns out", "the key is", "learned that", "the issue was", "root cause" **Memory Storage:** - Store in localStorage under key `agenticchat_memory` - Max 500 memories (FIFO eviction of lowest-confidence old ones) - Each memory has a confidence score (0-1) based on pattern match strength - Deduplication: skip if >80% word overlap with existing memory **Memory Recall (proactive):** - When a user types a message, scan stored memories for relevance - Use keyword overlap between input text and memory content - Show a subtle "🧠 Memories" indicator near the input when relevant memories found - Clicking it shows a floating panel with relevant memories ranked by relevance **Memory Browser Panel (Alt+Shift+Y):** - Full overlay panel showing all stored memories - Filter by type (fact/decision/preference/action/insight) - Search across all memories - Delete individual memories or clear all - Show source session name and timestamp - Export as JSON - Memory count and type breakdown stats at top **Integration points:** - Register with `CommandPalette.register()` - name: 'conversation memory', icon: '🧠' - Register with `KeyboardShortcuts.register()` - 'Alt+Shift+Y' - Register with `SlashCommands.register()` - '/memory' to open panel, '/remember ' to manually add - Use `SafeStorage` for localStorage access - Use `_escapeHtml()` for HTML escaping (global function available) - Use `_safeParse()` for JSON parsing (global function available) - After extraction, if visible, re-render **Auto-extraction hook:** - Use MutationObserver on `#chat-output` to detect new assistant messages - When a new `.msg.assistant-msg` appears, extract memories from the entire conversation - Debounce extraction to avoid running on every DOM change (500ms) ### 2. Add to `style.css` - append styles Add styles for: - `.memory-panel` - full overlay (fixed, z-index 10000, dark theme matching app) - `.memory-header` - header with title, search, filter tabs, close button - `.memory-filters` - type filter buttons (horizontal tabs) - `.memory-list` - scrollable memory cards - `.memory-card` - individual memory with type badge, content, source, actions - `.memory-type-badge` - colored badge per type (fact=blue, decision=green, preference=purple, action=orange, insight=teal) - `.memory-stats` - stats bar at top - `.memory-indicator` - subtle floating indicator near chat input - `.memory-recall-popup` - small popup showing relevant memories when typing - Use CSS variables: `var(--bg)`, `var(--fg)` for theme compatibility - All dark-theme default colors matching existing app (#1a1a2e, #16213e, etc.) ### 3. Add to `index.html` - add button in toolbar Find the toolbar div with `aria-label="Chat input"` and add this button before the closing ``: ```html ``` ## IMPORTANT CONSTRAINTS - Do NOT use any external dependencies - Do NOT modify any existing modules - Use the same coding style as existing modules (see ConversationStash, SessionLinker, ConversationMoodRing for reference) - The module must be a self-contained IIFE assigned to `var ConversationMemory` - All DOM creation should be done in JavaScript (no template literals with backticks - use string concatenation for wide compat) - Use `SafeStorage.get()` and `SafeStorage.set()` for localStorage - Use `_safeParse(json, default)` for JSON parsing - Use `_escapeHtml(str)` for escaping ## Verification After implementing, run: ``` node -c app.js ``` to verify JavaScript syntax is valid. Do NOT commit or push. Just make the code changes to app.js, style.css, and index.html.
I’m checking the existing app structure and the nearby modules you referenced so the new memory module matches the codebase’s patterns before I edit anything.

ToolUse.name: Run rg -n "ConversationStash|SessionLinker|ConversationMoodRing|CommandPalette\.register|KeyboardShortcuts\.register|SlashCommands\.register|SafeStorage|_escapeHtml|_safeParse|aria-label=\"Chat input\"|id=\"chat-output\"" app.js style.css index.html

content.Text: `rg` isn’t available here, so I’m falling back to targeted file reads and `Select-String` to locate the registration points, storage helpers, and module style I need to mirror.

ToolUse.name: Run Select-String -Path app.js -Pattern 'var ConversationStash = \(function \(\)|var SessionLinker = \(function \(\)|var ConversationMoodRing = \(function \(\)' -Context 0,80
